hero_title_accent: False Positive Case Routing for VASPs
hero_subtitle: SFTY helps compliance teams route KYT false positives based on risk and policy criteria to optimize review workflows and maintain a clear audit trail for transaction monitoring cases.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: False positive cases slow compliance reviews
problem_description: Compliance teams face delays when false positive alerts from transaction monitoring remain unresolved or routed inefficiently. This creates manual workload, inconsistent case handling and weak audit trails across KYT workflows for VASPs.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects KYT alerts, transaction risk data, policy rules and case status to automate false positive routing, streamline reviews and maintain a consistent audit trail for VASP compliance teams.
workflow_title: From wallet risk to false positive case routing
workflow_description: SFTY channels wallet risk alerts through policy-driven reviews and case workflows to prioritize false positives for timely escalation or clearance decisions.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented alerts. One case-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY organizes fragmented KYT alerts into structured cases, routing workflows, policy-based decisions and audit-ready evidence for clearer, faster resolution of false positives.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports VASPs with controlled routing of false positives, preserving audit-ready evidence and streamlining compliance workflows for bank due diligence and enterprise governance.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support routing false positives in KYT cases
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how false positive cases move through compliance workflows
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence supports decisions on KYT false positives
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how policy and risk guide false positive case routing
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for KYT compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects KYT alerts, wallet risk, transaction data and cases into one workflow, helping compliance teams manage false positives and triage risk with structured reviews and policy rules.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY route false positive cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes false positive cases based on configurable internal policies, risk thresholds and case status to assign reviews, escalate or close alerts following compliance workflows.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and all supporting evidence in audit-ready packages for review, regulatory inspection or internal governance audits.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with current KYT providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY works above existing KYT and blockchain analytics providers, connecting outputs into connected workflows without requiring provider replacement.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace transaction monitoring providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace transaction monitoring providers but connects their outputs into structured workflows, cases, evidence and reports for better compliance control.
final_title: Route KYT false positives into controlled cases
final_description: Review how SFTY helps compliance teams route false positives by risk and policy for clearer case workflow and audit-ready evidence.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
